CellGraphR 0.99.0
  • Reference
    • Visualizing Cell Spatial Graphs
    • Analyzing Cell Graphs with igraph
    • Multi-modal Analysis: Cell-Graph Features with Genomic and Clinical Data
    • EDA: Graph-based Features from Spatial Cell Data

Visualize a cell spatial graph

Source: R/plotGraph.R
plotGraph.Rd

Renders an igraph object as a ggplot2 figure. Nodes are positioned at their spatial coordinates (auto-detected from vertex attributes) or, if no coordinate columns are found, using a Fruchterman-Reingold layout. Nodes can be coloured by any vertex attribute.

Usage

plotGraph(
  g,
  x_col = NULL,
  y_col = NULL,
  color_by = NULL,
  title = NULL,
  node_size = 1,
  edge_alpha = 0.3,
  image = NULL,
  image_scale = 1,
  wsi_dim = NULL
)

Arguments

g

An undirected igraph object, typically produced by importGraphH5 or buildSpatialGraph.

x_col

Character(1) or NULL. Vertex attribute name for x coordinates. When NULL (default) the function probes common names "x", "cx", "centroid_x", "X" in that order and falls back to a force-directed layout if none are found.

y_col

Character(1) or NULL. Same as x_col for y coordinates ("y", "cy", "centroid_y", "Y").

color_by

Character(1) or NULL. Vertex attribute used to colour nodes. When NULL (default) the function probes "cell_type", "type", "label".

title

Character(1) or NULL. Plot title. Defaults to the patient_id graph attribute when available.

node_size

Numeric(1). Point size passed to geom_point. Default 1.

edge_alpha

Numeric(1) in \([0,1]\). Transparency of edges. Default 0.3.

image

Character(1) file path to a source image (PNG, JPEG, SVS, NDPI, TIFF) or a pre-loaded raster array (e.g. from png::readPNG). When provided the image is rendered as a background layer beneath the graph. Default NULL (no image).

image_scale

Numeric(1). Scale factor applied to vertex coordinates before overlaying on the image. Use this when the graph coordinates and image pixels are at different resolutions (e.g. 0.5 if the image is half the resolution of the coordinate space). Default 1 (no scaling).

wsi_dim

Numeric(2) or NULL. Width and height of the original whole-slide image in pixels, c(width, height). When provided, the exact scale factors image_width / wsi_width and image_height / wsi_height are used to map graph coordinates (in WSI pixel space) to the thumbnail image. This gives pixel-accurate alignment and is recommended over image_scale when the WSI dimensions are known. Default NULL (auto-detect).

Value

A ggplot object.

Examples

set.seed(42)
nuclei <- lapply(seq_len(40), function(i)
    list(centroid = runif(2, 0, 500),
         type     = sample(c("neoplastic", "stromal", "immune"), 1)))

g <- buildSpatialGraph(nuclei, type = "delaunay")
plotGraph(g)

plotGraph(g, color_by = "type", title = "Delaunay graph")
